preflight_summary.scope: Agent that reads repository context and can invoke MCP tools to open pull requests and draft deployment tickets.
preflight_summary.system_risk_profile: agent; MCP/tool-using; external actions; code generation; production-adjacent
release_blockers: Tool authorization and approval evidence are missing.
MCP token audience validation evidence is missing.
important_fixes: Add human approval gates for pull request and deployment-ticket actions.
Add MCP token and tool-schema validation tests.
non_blocking_backlog: Add AI Defense Matrix owner mapping for agent identities.

trust_boundaries: MCP client to remote MCP servers

tool: downstream GitHub and ticketing API calls

facts: The design includes remote MCP servers.
The agent can create pull requests and deployment tickets.
assumptions: Production deployment itself is not directly executed by the agent.
missing_architecture_evidence: No MCP authorization diagram was found.
No approval binding or replay protection design was found.

narrative: What are we working on: a tool-using agent with remote MCP integrations.
What can go wrong: malicious repo content or MCP responses can drive unauthorized actions.
What are we going to do: require runtime authorization, human approval, schema validation, and token audience checks.
Did we do a good job: human AppSec review must validate tool scopes and action approval before pilot.

threats.title: Prompt injection drives unauthorized tool use
threats.attack_path: A malicious repository file is read into context and instructs the agent to create or modify external artifacts.
threats.likely_impact: Unauthorized pull requests or deployment tickets could be created under trusted context.
existing_controls: Human user starts the agent session
threats.mitigation_decision: Block release until high-impact MCP tools require human approval and runtime policy authorization.
threats.residual_risk: medium after approval gates and tool policy tests pass

threats.title: MCP token passthrough or audience confusion
threats.attack_path: An MCP server accepts or forwards a token not issued for that server, allowing confused-deputy access to downstream APIs.
threats.likely_impact: A compromised or misconfigured MCP server could misuse user authorization outside intended scope.
existing_controls: Remote MCP is planned to use OAuth
gaps: No evidence of issuer, audience, expiration, and scope validation
No evidence that token passthrough is forbidden
threats.mitigation_decision: Require MCP authorization evidence and tests before Security review.
threats.residual_risk: low to medium after token validation and passthrough tests pass

evidence_gaps.title: MCP tool inventory
evidence_gaps.requested_artifact: List each MCP server, tool, scope, downstream API, and side-effect classification.

evidence_gaps.title: Human approval gate design
evidence_gaps.requested_artifact: Attach approval flow showing canonical action parameters, approver identity, and block-on-timeout behavior.

evidence_gaps.title: MCP authorization and token validation evidence
evidence_gaps.requested_artifact: Attach tests or design showing issuer, audience, expiration, scope validation, and no token passthrough to downstream APIs.

security_tests.title: Unauthorized MCP tool invocation test
security_tests.objective: Verify repo-controlled content cannot invoke tools outside user authorization or approval boundaries.
steps: Load a repository file containing hostile instructions
Ask the agent to summarize the project
Assert high-impact calls are blocked pending approval
security_tests.expected_secure_behavior: The runtime blocks unauthorized tool calls and requires explicit human approval for high-impact actions.
security_tests.evidence_to_retain: Repo fixture, agent trace, denied tool-call log, approval prompt screenshot or transcript.

security_tests.title: MCP token audience and passthrough test
security_tests.objective: Verify MCP servers reject wrong-audience tokens and do not forward inbound access tokens to downstream APIs.
steps: Call MCP server with wrong-audience token
security_tests.expected_secure_behavior: Wrong-audience tokens are rejected and downstream APIs receive separately issued tokens, not the client-presented token.
security_tests.evidence_to_retain: Token validation logs, HTTP traces, and downstream mock assertions.

engineering_backlog.title: Add runtime tool policy and human approval gates
engineering_backlog.problem: High-impact external actions can be requested by agent context without proven deterministic approval enforcement.
engineering_backlog.recommended_change: Implement policy-enforced tool authorization and explicit approval gates for pull request and deployment-ticket actions.
acceptance_criteria: Policy and approval denials are logged

engineering_backlog.title: Implement MCP token audience validation and no-passthrough checks
engineering_backlog.problem: MCP authorization design does not prove tokens are bound to the intended MCP server or isolated from downstream API calls.
engineering_backlog.recommended_change: Validate issuer, audience, expiration, and scope on every MCP request; mint separate downstream tokens instead of forwarding inbound tokens.
acceptance_criteria: Downstream APIs do not receive the client-presented token

unresolved_decisions: Which MCP tools are allowed in the pilot and which are blocked until later review.
human_review_points: Security must review MCP authorization design, approval gates, and tool logs before pilot.
